items.title: Simeone spreekt boze Oblak tegen na afstraffing Atlético
items.summary: Was het o zo zwakke optreden van Atlético Madrid zondagmiddag tegen degradatiekandidaat Rayo Vallecano een mentale kwestie of niet? Een halve week na de indrukwekkende zege op Barcelona verloor de topclub met 3-0 van de stadgenoot. Trainer Diego Simeone was het niet eens met de conclusie van zijn doelman Jan Oblak.

items.title: VI Live: Napoli weer naast AS Roma ondanks trefzekere Malen
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Purpose and togetherness: What the soccer world can learn from FA Cup heroes Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield produced one of soccer's greatest Cinderella stories in beating Crystal Palace in the FA Cup, but the tale behind the triumph is even more remarkable.

items.title: After a dramatic Scottish Premiership weekend, where do we stand now?
items.summary: Four teams in the title race and three points separating the top three - this was a weekend at the top of the Scottish Premiership that swung the race towards a 'race to the wire' contest.

items.title: Arteta: Injury-hit Arsenal 'need some players back'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta admitted a mounting injury list could leave Arsenal vulnerable in their pursuit of silverware this season.

items.title: No notes: FA Cup GK is hero despite research fail
items.summary: Leeds United's Lucas Perri was the penalty shootout hero despite his preparation being foiled by Birmingham City.

items.title: FA Cup 'mud bath' survived by PL strugglers
items.summary: Wolves boss Rob Edwards described his side's 1-0 FA Cup win at Grimsby as an "aggressive game of headers and volleys" as the Premier League club survived a mud bath.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: Tengstedt zou late strafschop helemaal niet nemen bij Feyenoord
items.summary: Casper Tengstedt kroonde zich zondagmiddag tot matchwinner bij Feyenoord door de penalty binnen te schieten in blessuretijd tegen Go Ahead Eagles . Maar de Deen was eigenlijk helemaal niet de aangewezen man om penalty te nemen.

items.title: Flick heeft 'beste speler ter wereld' weer terug bij Barcelona
items.summary: Barcelona kan maandagavond weer beschikken over Raphinha. De Braziliaan kwam in februari nog niet in actie voor Barcelona vanwege blessureleed, maar kan maandagavond tegen Girona weer minuten maken, zo liet trainer Hansi Flick zondag weten tijdens de persconferentie.

items.title: De Minaur reigns in Rotterdam at third time of asking
items.summary: Alex de Minaur claimed his 11th ATP title after beating Felix Auger-Aliassime in straight sets in the final of the Rotterdam Open.

items.title: The big VAR debate: with, without or change its scope?
items.summary: After a weekend of FA Cup action without VAR, BBC Sport ask managers, fans and pundits whether the game needs technology - and what needs to change.

items.title: Spurs unstoppable in 10-goal thriller against Aston Villa
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur put 7 goals past Aston Villa to keep themselves in the running for a Champions League place.

items.title: Kökcü scoort voor vijfde keer op rij bij winnend Besiktas
items.summary: Orkun Kökcü heeft zijn doelpuntenreeks zondagavond een vervolg gegeven. De captain van Besiktas sloeg toe in de uitwedstrijd tegen stadgenoot Istanbul Basaksehir, dat in de zesde minuut van de extra tijd uiteindelijk de genadeklap incasseerde.

items.title: 183 team changes in 14 ties - but does it really matter for the FA Cup?
items.summary: Does 183 team changes in 14 ties devalue the FA Cup? Chief football writer Phil McNulty insists the cup retains its old magic.

items.title: Celtic spirit reminds O'Neill of glory years
items.summary: Interim manager Martin O'Neill says the current side's fighting spirit reminds him of his previous Celtic glory years after their comeback win in Kilmarnock.

items.title: Eberechi Eze inspires Arsenal to emphatic FA Cup victory against Wigan
items.summary: It has been a testing few months for the man who scored the winner for Crystal Palace in last season’s FA Cup final. But after being hooked at half-time during the disappointing draw with Brentford on Thursday when Mikel Arteta said he is still adapting to life in north London, perhaps this competition could help breathe new life into Eberechi Eze’s Arsenal career. As well as providing assists for Noni Madueke and Gabriel Martinelli’s opening goals – albeit against a poor Wigan side who are languishing in League One’s relegation zone – the England midfielder’s swagger was back for the first time since he scored a hat-trick in the north London derby in November. Continue reading...

items.title: Youssef Chermiti hat-trick powers Rangers to victory over leaders Hearts
items.summary: This proved the game of Scotland’s top-flight season. The seriousness given to it by Rangers, plus their scale of full-time celebration, said much about the progress of their opposition. Hearts’ lead at the summit has been cut to two after the Edinburgh side lost out by two goals. As the scoreline suggests, this was pulsating stuff. Youssef Chermiti was once ridiculed in these parts. His £8m arrival for Everton was used as a stick with which to beat Kevin Thelwell, the now departed sporting director. Chermiti’s hat-trick against Hearts, added to a recent double at Celtic Park, means he at least has a useful knack of standing up on the big occasion. Rangers’ next task is simple; to build on recent progress and overhaul Hearts. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Super Sunday' - Weston and Stoecker win mixed skeleton gold for Team GB
items.summary: Matt Weston and Tabby Stoecker teamed up to claim Olympic gold in the mixed team skeleton competition.

items.title: 'A throwback' - Wolves beat Grimsby in FA Cup mudbath
items.summary: Premier League Wolves edge League Two Grimsby in an "old school cup tie" which provided an FA Cup "throwback".
